whatThisIs: A staging reference for accidental hypothermia by core temperature: mild (35-32 C / 95-90 F - shivering, intact mentation), moderate (32-28 C / 90-82 F - shivering wanes, progressive obtundation, J waves possible, increasing arrhythmia risk), and severe (<28 C / <82 F - shivering absent, coma, profound bradycardia, ventricular irritability). Sophie Well shows the temperature thresholds, the expected clinical findings at each stage, and the rewarming approach typically associated with the stage (passive vs. active external vs. active internal / extracorporeal).
whenToUse: Use this in a wilderness, EMS, or ED setting whenever a patient has been exposed to cold and the disposition or rewarming strategy turns on the stage. Two operational rules: a low-reading thermometer is required (a standard ED thermometer often stops at 34 C / 93 F and will under-call severity), and severe hypothermia warrants extreme handling care - any rough movement or excessive chest compressions on a stiff bradycardic patient can precipitate refractory ventricular fibrillation. "Nobody is dead until they are warm and dead" remains the operating principle; full resuscitation continues during rewarming to a core temperature above 32 C unless there are obvious lethal injuries.
